title: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description: The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework designed for processing large data sets across clusters of computers. It consists of two main components: the Map component and the Reduce component. The Map component breaks down the input data into smaller chunks and processes them in parallel, while the Reduce component aggregates the results and produces the final output. The architecture is scalable, fault-tolerant, and provides high-throughput data processing capabilities. It uses the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) for distributed storage and MapReduce for distributed processing. The architecture is widely used in big data analytics and data-intensive computing applications.
raw_response:
TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture

DESCRIPTION:
The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework designed for processing large data sets across clusters of computers. It consists of two main components: the Map component and the Reduce component. The Map component breaks down the input data into smaller chunks and processes them in parallel, while the Reduce component aggregates the results and produces the final output. The architecture is scalable, fault-tolerant, and provides high-throughput data processing capabilities. It uses the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) for distributed storage and MapReduce for distributed processing. The architecture is widely used in big data analytics and data-intensive computing applications.
